incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-41940 (CVSS 9.8) in cPanel/WHM since at least February, granting full host control.
incidents.impact: CISA ordered all federal agencies to patch by May 3; major hosting providers (Hosting.com, Namecheap, KnownHost, HostPapa, InMotion) firewalled customers off their own products. Thousands of cPanel instances exposed; potential mass website takeover, data theft, and service disruption.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into Moldova's national healthcare database; specifics not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Personal and financial data on virtually every adult Moldovan citizen, foreign insurance contributors, and private medical providers stolen; ~30% of agency data impacted. No ransom demanded. Comes amid Russian threats of military intervention against Moldova.

incidents.vector: Sophisticated social engineering and exploitation of single-points-of-trust in decentralized finance infrastructure, increasingly accelerated by AI tooling.
incidents.impact: TRM Labs reports North Korea now holds 76% of all stolen crypto in 2026 — nearly all from two attacks (Drift Protocol $285M April 1, KelpDAO $292M April 18) totaling $575M in 18 days, funding the DPRK weapons program.

incidents.vector: Misconfigured Medicare portal database exposed Social Security numbers of U.S. health providers.
incidents.impact: Sensitive provider PII including SSNs exposed in the federal Medicare provider portal; scope of who accessed the data not yet disclosed.

incidents.vector: Four npm packages in SAP's Cloud Application Programming model (@cap-js/sqlite, @cap-js/postgres, @cap-js/db-service, mbt) compromised with a malicious preinstall hook that deploys a wormable credential stealer using the Bun runtime; identical toolchain to the @bitwarden/cli compromise ("Mini Shai-Hulud").
incidents.impact: ~570K weekly downloads affected; payload harvests GitHub/npm tokens, AWS/Azure/GCP secrets, Kubernetes tokens, and self-propagates by republishing legitimate packages. Targets enterprise CI/CD pipelines and cloud credentials.

incidents.vector: Mirai-variant botnet built from TP-Link Archer AX21 routers vulnerable to CVE-2023-1389; ran from infrastructure of Brazilian DDoS-protection firm Huge Networks after a January 2026 SSH-key compromise of the CEO.
incidents.impact: Multi-year campaign of massive DDoS attacks against Brazilian ISPs, leveraging DNS reflection/amplification and tens of thousands of compromised devices. Huge Networks engaged forensics; CEO claims competitor framing.

incidents.victim: U.S. federal agencies and Cisco firewall users globally
incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA/Firepower devices to deploy custom "FIRESTARTER" backdoor; persists across firmware patches and software reboots, removable only by hard reboot or device reimage.
incidents.impact: CISA discovered the implant on a federal civilian agency device months after September 2025 patches were applied; updated Emergency Directive 25-03 mandates federal audit and memory snapshots. Impacts Firepower 1000/2100/4100/9300 and Secure Firewall 1200/3100/4200 series. Continuation of an espionage-focused campaign on perimeter network devices.

incidents.victim: Cuban embassy in Washington, D.C. (and ~5,000 Exchange servers)
incidents.vector: Compromise of on-premises Microsoft Exchange email server; same actor behind a broader campaign hitting more than 5,000 Exchange servers this year.
incidents.impact: Diplomatic email inboxes stolen, reportedly to monitor U.S. naval blockade activity; underscores continued risk of unpatched/legacy Exchange deployments worldwide.

incidents.victim: Worldwide SOHO router and IoT device owners
incidents.vector: Large-scale covert proxy networks built from compromised SOHO routers and IoT devices (e.g., a botnet of 200,000 devices), maintained by Chinese information security companies and used to disguise malicious traffic.
incidents.impact: Joint advisory from 12 allied agencies (US, UK, Australia, Canada, Germany, Netherlands, NZ, Japan, Spain, Sweden) describes how China-nexus actors now use these networks for reconnaissance, malware delivery, and pre-positioning on critical infrastructure.

incidents.victim: Bitwarden npm consumers and Checkmarx ecosystem
incidents.vector: Malicious @bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0 npm package with multi-stage payload; coordinated compromise of Checkmarx Docker Hub images, GitHub Actions, and VS Code extensions sharing C2 infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Wormable credential theft from cloud providers, CI/CD systems, and developer workstations; backdoors every npm package the victim can publish. "Shai-Hulud: The Third Coming" wave.

incidents.vector: Phishing campaign using Softr AI-powered web app builder to rapidly generate credential-harvesting pages targeting Microsoft Exchange/OWA logins; first time Cisco Talos has documented an AI tool used in a Talos IR phishing case.
incidents.impact: Credential harvesting against public administration accounts; demonstrates how AI tools collapse the barrier to entry for credential theft campaigns. Public administration was the most targeted vertical for the third straight quarter (24% of Talos IR engagements).

incidents.victim: Russian aviation industry and government agencies
incidents.vector: Phishing emails with infected archives, malvertising mimicking aviation software vendors, and fake GearUP / FPV drone simulator / Starlink-bypass installers hosted on SourceForge.
incidents.impact: Theft of GIS/geospatial data on Russian roads, infrastructure, and potentially strategic facilities; harvesting of screenshots, keystrokes, browser data, Telegram credentials, and device location. Active since at least September 2025.

incidents.vector: Lumma Stealer infection of a Context.ai employee in February (via search for Roblox cheats) gave attackers Context.ai's AWS environment and OAuth tokens — including a Vercel employee's Google Workspace token — used to take over the Vercel Google account and pivot to Vercel environment variables.
incidents.impact: Limited Vercel customers had AWS keys, source code, and database access exposed; stolen data offered for sale online. Highlights interconnected SaaS/OAuth integration risk; Vercel CEO suspects AI-accelerated tradecraft. CrowdStrike and Mandiant assisting.

incidents.vector: "ZionSiphon" OT-targeting malware designed to scan for Israeli water-management software, then push pump pressure and chlorine levels to maximum settings. Politically charged strings reference "poisoning the population of Tel Aviv and Haifa."
incidents.impact: Failed to trigger due to a XOR encoding bug in the Israeli IP-range check; deletes itself thinking it landed outside Israel. Continues a pattern of Iranian OT attacks on Israeli water systems since 2020.

incidents.victim: ANTS — French national identity documents agency
incidents.vector: Breach of the French government agency that manages national identity documents, driving licenses, and vehicle registrations; specifics not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Stolen French citizen identity records advertised for sale online; potentially affects millions of French residents.

incidents.vector: Same campaign believed to be tied to compromised SaaS OAuth tokens and infostealer-based credential theft in connected SaaS ecosystems.
incidents.impact: Carnival data on 7.5 million customers leaked publicly; multiple Fortune-class brands (Zara fashion, 7-Eleven, Udemy, Medtronic medical devices) also impacted in the same wave.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack of unspecified origin forced the city to take its IT network offline; surrounding Leon County disconnected its network as a precaution — language consistent with a ransomware incident.
incidents.impact: Multiple public-facing city services and websites unavailable; ongoing recovery and forensic work disclosed by city IT.

incidents.vector: Sophisticated multi-day distributed denial-of-service campaign that evolved through several mitigation rounds.
incidents.impact: Intermittent outages across the Bluesky platform for nearly a day; follow-on DDoS attempts continued through the week but were mitigated. Mastodon's flagship server was also hit by a separate DDoS in the same period.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to internal corporate systems; specifics not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Itron disclosed and contained the breach; cyber insurance expected to cover most costs. Itron supplies metering and grid technology to U.S. utilities, raising downstream concerns even though the company says no customer-facing systems were impacted.

incidents.victim: Unnamed organization — Crimson Collective intrusion (Talos IR case)
incidents.vector: GitHub Personal Access Token leaked on a public website went unnoticed for months; attacker used legitimate tools (TruffleHog) to scan thousands of repos for additional secrets, found Azure client secrets, then used Microsoft Graph API to authenticate and exfiltrate data from Azure cloud storage.
incidents.impact: First Talos IR engagement against the Crimson Collective extortion group (active since September 2025); attacker also attempted to inject secret-harvesting code into multiple GitHub repos. Demonstrates the rising risk of "living-off-the-cloud" attacks using native APIs and legitimate developer tooling.
